creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): still reproduces. Sorting a list containing NaN produces a nonsensical order because NaN violates comparison transitivity.

**Repro:**
nan = pow(-1, 0.5)   // NaN  (note: 0.0/0 now raises RAD20036 divide-by-zero, so use pow)
items = [5, 4, nan, 3, nan, 2, 1]
print(items.sort())  // broken order - NaN not sorted to end, comparisons inconsistent

**Design options:** error on NaN in sort, or define a NaN ordering (e.g. sort NaN to the end).
title: NaN poisons list sorting - produces completely broken order
